picks.title: Bloodywood
picks.blurb: Heavy-metal collective Bloodywood brings visceral energy to Melkweg's smaller stage Sunday.

picks.title: Guns N' Roses and Mammoth
picks.blurb: Rock legends Guns N' Roses headline Amsterdam's biggest indoor venue this Saturday.

picks.title: Fabrique des Lumières: Master of Impressionism
picks.blurb: Immersive light projection show of Impressionist masters. Last weekend to catch this digital art experience.

picks.title: Ed van der Elsken photography exhibition
picks.blurb: Dutch photographer's 20th-century images open this weekend. Powerful, historical documentary work.
picks.why: Opens Saturday 19 June, runs through 13 September.

picks.title: 24 uur Nieuw-West
picks.blurb: District-wide open doors event with free activities: sports, creativity, nature, food, community gathering throughout Nieuw-West.

picks.title: Jodeci
picks.blurb: R&B legends Jodeci perform at Amsterdam's mid-sized live venue Saturday.

picks.title: Celeste
picks.blurb: British soul-pop rising star Celeste plays Paradiso's main hall Saturday evening.
picks.why: Contemporary soul artist, Paradiso's main stage

picks.title: Kármán x Sbvrs
picks.blurb: Daytime outdoor garden DJ set with mixed electronic sounds in summer setting.

picks.title: Royal Holland Cup (Roei)
picks.blurb: International rowing races with 20+ countries. Festival atmosphere with food, drink, live music and spectator seating.

picks.title: LuminAir Rooftop Bar
picks.venue: DoubleTree by Hilton, near Central Station
picks.blurb: High-vibe elevated sky bar with 360° city views. Father's Day special: complimentary whisky for dads.
picks.why: Sunset drinks with views, family-friendly weekend special

picks.title: Jardin Rouge Festival
picks.blurb: Multi-day music festival spanning the weekend with varied acts at De Roode Bioscoop.

picks.title: Seaport Days Festival (Havenfestival)
picks.venue: Java Island / Kop van het Java-eiland
picks.blurb: Free maritime festival with ship visits, demo tours, boat rides, maritime activities, and Waterstof Inspirience.

picks.title: Diageo World Class Cocktail Festival Rotterdam
picks.blurb: International cocktail competition and festival with master bartenders. Runs through early July but kicks off this weekend.

picks.title: Finale Koninklijk Concertgebouw Concours 2026
picks.blurb: Six mini-concerts showcasing young classical musicians competing in this prestigious finals competition.
picks.why: Elite music competition finale, inspiring performances

picks.title: Eye(s) Open: New Perspectives on Colonial Film Heritage
picks.when: Sat 20 Jun – Sun 21 Jun (ongoing until 6 Sep)
picks.blurb: Eleven contemporary artists respond to Eye's colonial-era film collection from Indonesia and Suriname with new commissioned works.

picks.title: Van Gogh Museum: In Search of Stability
picks.blurb: Largest Van Gogh collection in the world, plus summer exhibition on the artist's path to fame.
picks.why: Essential Amsterdam museum, always worth visiting.

picks.title: World Press Photo
picks.blurb: Annual showcase of world's most compelling photojournalism and documentary photography, running through September.

picks.title: Canal Booze Cruise
picks.blurb: 60–90 minute guided open-boat cruise with drinks, live crew, and views of UNESCO canals and seven bridges.
picks.why: Iconic summer activity, flexible weekend slots

picks.title: Dahn Vo and Kho Liang Ie exhibitions
picks.blurb: Major retrospective of functionalist furniture design, plus avant-garde Vietnamese-born artist Dahn Vo.

picks.title: Bite of Amsterdam 2026
picks.blurb: Food festival featuring local chefs, street food vendors, and tastings across the city. Three days of culinary discovery.
picks.why: Major food event; attracts serious foodies
picks.source: Your Little Black Book · Bite of Amsterdam / I amsterdam · Bite of Amsterdam official / I amsterdam · Bite of Amsterdam official · I amsterdam official calendar

picks.title: Open Garden Days
picks.blurb: Explore 30+ normally private canal gardens across Amsterdam; boat shuttle connects sites. Hidden gems rarely seen from street.
picks.source: I amsterdam · I amsterdam, AmsterdamTips · Open Garden Days official · I amsterdam culture guide · I amsterdam / Amsterdamian

picks.title: Holland Festival: Benjamin Hulett & Carina Vinke
picks.blurb: Holland Festival classical performance by celebrated singers Benjamin Hulett and Carina Vinke at world-renowned Concertgebouw.
picks.source: I amsterdam · Songkick, Holland Festival · Songkick

picks.title: Bacchus Wine Festival
picks.blurb: Wine tastings, food pairings, live music and entertainment in forest setting. Amsterdam's oldest wine event running three weekends.
picks.source: I amsterdam · Amsterdam Spotted / Bacchus Wijnfestival · Amsterdamian

picks.title: Museum Market
picks.blurb: Third Sunday of month: artisan makers, crafts, handmade goods, food stalls and live music in leafy museum plaza.

picks.title: The Movies — arthouse cinema
picks.blurb: The Netherlands’ oldest cinema still running — Art Deco rooms, a dinner-and-a-film deal, arthouse bills.
picks.why: Indoor · dinner + a film · perfect when it’s wet

picks.title: Hortus Botanicus
picks.blurb: One of the world’s oldest botanical gardens — a butterfly greenhouse and three-climate glasshouse to wander.
picks.source: I amsterdam Weekend Guide · De Hortus

picks.title: East Beach — rooftop city beach
picks.blurb: A new 800m² rooftop city beach above Oosterpark — sand, beach chairs, drinks, open to all.

picks.title: Carel Visser — sculpture in the gardens
picks.blurb: Free outdoor sculpture show — the first artist solo in the Rijksmuseum gardens, opening this weekend.

picks.title: De Pimpelmees
picks.blurb: Cosy neighbourhood wine bar — European wines from €6, Italian snacks, free chips. Opened early 2026.

picks.title: Yumna Al-Arashi — Body as Resistance
picks.blurb: First solo museum show for Al-Arashi — photography, sculpture and book confront the policing of women’s bodies.

picks.title: GRIMM (8+)
picks.blurb: Hip-hop and classical ballet collide in a wild reimagining of classic fairytales—dynamic, darker, funnier.
picks.why: Spectacular family dance show, critics' pick

picks.title: 24 Hours Nieuw-West
picks.blurb: District-wide free celebration: Van Eestern Museum parties, brewery tours, street art, bird watching, DJ sets, workshops.

picks.title: Spinoza — De Mokum Musical
picks.blurb: Open-air musical about philosopher Spinoza set in old Amsterdam, in the forest theatre.
picks.why: Cross-source (LBB + I amsterdam) · open-air · dry-night plan

picks.title: Escape Club: deLux Ydro
picks.blurb: Late-night dance with Ydro, Irwan, Kimberlee Ramirez, Jay Junior plus weekly guests.
picks.why: Sunday night after-hours with rotation DJs

picks.title: Havenfestival 2026
picks.blurb: Bustling harbour island packed with free activities, demonstrations, and entertainment for all ages.

picks.title: As
picks.blurb: Wood-fired, ingredient-led cooking and natural wine in a striking round garden pavilion.

picks.title: BAK
picks.blurb: A light-filled warehouse with an open kitchen and an art-covered wall; a set seasonal menu of produce-driven dishes.

picks.title: Choux
picks.blurb: Inventive, veg-forward small plates and natural wine in a raw red-brick space near the IJ.

picks.title: Moise
picks.blurb: An understated De Pijp boutique with a razor-sharp eye — Acne, Lemaire, Marant and the like, hand-picked and beautifully shown.

picks.title: Patta
picks.blurb: The homegrown streetwear label and culture hub — drops, sneakers and the community that put Amsterdam on the map.

picks.title: Pllek — waterfront hang
picks.blurb: A cargo-container café-bar on the NDSM wharf — big windows onto the IJ, sofas, and a wood stove when it turns.
picks.why: Indoor seating · IJ views · cozy when grey

picks.title: RIJKS®
picks.blurb: Joris Bijdendijk’s Michelin-starred dining room beside the Rijksmuseum — the “cuisine of the Low Countries”, done with flair.

picks.title: X Bank
picks.blurb: A concept store in a former bank showing 180+ Dutch fashion, design and art labels — couture to hand-thrown ceramics.
picks.why: Best of Dutch design · one room

picks.title: Rijsel
picks.blurb: Lille-inspired French cooking in a converted school — that rotisserie chicken is the order.

picks.title: Bar Fisk
picks.blurb: Sun-soaked Mediterranean seafood and sharing plates in De Pijp.

picks.title: FC Hyena
picks.blurb: An arthouse cinema with a wine bar and a small kitchen, right on the IJ in Noord.

picks.title: Haarlem — the easy escape
picks.blurb: A calmer historic city 15 min away — Grote Markt, the St-Bavo organ, a Saturday market and café-lined square.

picks.title: Mobilia
picks.blurb: Three floors of high design on the Utrechtsestraat — icons by the masters alongside more attainable covetables.

picks.title: Vlieger
picks.blurb: A two-floor temple to paper since 1869 — hundreds of colours, handmade sheets and card stacked to the ceiling.

picks.title: Café Chris — oldest brown café
picks.blurb: Open since 1624: a creaky, golden-lit Jordaan brown café for a slow afternoon beer while it pours outside.

picks.title: Efteling
picks.blurb: Europe’s oldest fairytale theme park — enchanted forest, dark rides and proper coasters in a storybook world.

picks.title: Kriterion
picks.blurb: A legendary student-run cinema and café since 1945 — arthouse programming with a buzzy terrace.

picks.title: Madurodam
picks.blurb: The whole of the Netherlands at 1:25 — miniature canals, Schiphol and windmills kids can tower over.

picks.title: Sauna Deco — Art Deco baths
picks.blurb: A 1920s Art Deco bathhouse (salvaged from a Paris department store) with saunas, steam and a quiet tea room.
picks.why: Warm · restorative · the ultimate cold-and-wet antidote

picks.title: Friday Next
picks.blurb: A concept store and café in one — young Dutch design to browse, with brunch and coffee while you do it.

picks.title: WonderWood
picks.blurb: A gallery-shop devoted to plywood and mid-century modern — bentwood chairs and design classics since 1999.

picks.title: Eye Classics
picks.blurb: Film history on the big screen: restored classics screened in 35mm whenever possible.
picks.why: Ongoing programme; check schedule for weekend titles

picks.title: De Filmhallen
picks.blurb: A nine-screen arthouse cinema in a converted tram depot — with a bustling food hall right next door.
picks.why: Arthouse · in the old tram depot

picks.title: Eye Filmmuseum — repertory screening
picks.blurb: Across the IJ by free ferry: a striking film museum with a daily repertory programme and a river-view café.
picks.why: Indoor · a film + the ferry ride · weather-proof

picks.title: Cowboys to Catwalk
picks.blurb: A treasure-trove of vintage designer — Margiela, Marc Jacobs and one-off finds rummaged from decades of fashion.

picks.title: Zaanse Schans windmills
picks.blurb: Eight working windmills, cheese and crafts on the Zaan — free grounds, ~17 min train from Centraal.
picks.why: Short train · outdoor · classic kid day
